factors.name: US-Iran Diplomacy & Geopolitical De-escalation
factors.summary: US waives Iran oil sanctions; VP Vance reports 'good foundation' for final deal; Iranian oil flooding market. Reduced geopolitical premium typically weighs on safe-haven demand, including gold. Reuters/Bloomberg confirm 30M barrels shipped and IAEA inspector access agreed.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: WTI fell below $74/bbl on Iran sanctions relief; Brent weakness also evident. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations = lower real yields pressure removed but also signals growth concerns, mixed signal for gold.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations & Policy Stance
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley's Gower states Fed expected to hold rates through 2026, contrasting market expectations of ~1.6 hikes by year-end. This hawkish-hold posture supports higher real yields if inflation stays sticky, which would weigh on gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index Strength
factors.summary: EUR/USD trading near three-month lows (~1.1426); market expectations of hawkish Fed underpin USD despite geopolitical easing. Strong USD is typically inverse to gold; DXY momentum not in gold's favor.

factors.name: Tech/Equity Risk-Off (Google, SpaceX, Netflix declines)
factors.summary: Alphabet down ~5.3% on AI talent exodus (Bloomberg, 16:47); Netflix down 5.6% to lowest since Oct 2024; SpaceX down 10%+. Risk-off sentiment can support safe-haven flows to gold, but equities weakness is only moderate.

factors.name: Real Yields Trend
factors.summary: No fresh US inflation data (CPI/PCE) in news window. Fed hold-through-2026 stance could support elevated real yields if inflation remains contained. US PCE data coming up (mentioned in AUD/USD context) but not yet released; awaiting this catalyst.

factors.name: UK Political Shock (Starmer resignation)
factors.summary: Keir Starmer exits; Andy Burnham likely next PM. GBP rallied calmly, suggesting market view is orderly transition. Minor safe-haven demand spillover; not a major catalyst for gold.

factors.name: 4h Price Action & Recent Momentum
factors.summary: 4h change: -0.32%; 24h change: +0.10%. Essentially flat; price has NOT extended meaningfully in either direction yet. Move is early/uncertain.

screening.narrative: Gold faces a tug-of-war between conflicting forces. US-Iran de-escalation and resulting oil weakness reduce geopolitical risk premium and near-term inflation expectations, favoring a softer gold outlook. Simultaneously, a hawkish Fed hold (Morgan Stanley) and a strong USD (EUR/USD at three-month lows) are creating headwinds to real yields expansion, which typically caps gold upside. Tech selloff (Alphabet -5.3%, Netflix -5.6%) provides modest safe-haven support, but the move is not yet conviction-driven. Upcoming PCE inflation data will be critical to reset real yields expectations; absent that, gold is directionless.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation data (timing in news window suggests imminent release, likely this week)
Marco Rubio Middle East visit June 23-25 (Iran/Hormuz talks follow-up)
Fed speakers (Williams, Waller commentary on new Warsh regime if any)
Oil market reaction to Iran sanctions relief window (60-day pause through August)
Tech earnings season ramifications (Google, Meta, Super Micro volatility)
screening.risk_warning: If PCE data comes in hotter than expected, real yields rise sharply and gold could break lower. Conversely, if Iran deal collapses or Middle East tensions resurface, geopolitical premium re-enters and gold rallies. USD strength (EUR/USD breakdown below 1.1417 would signal fresh dollar leg) would also pressure gold; watch for breaks there. BoJ intervention risk (USD/JPY pulled from 161.92 near 161.07) could trigger yen carry unwind, potentially supporting risk assets over gold.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is early and flat; avoid chasing either direction. Wait for PCE release or a clear break below $4,180 (support) or above $4,225 (resistance) on increased volume before committing. If risk-off deepens (tech selloff accelerates), gold could find bids; if Iran deal progresses further and oil breaks $70, remain cautious on gold upside.
